data.narrative:
Ocius Technology is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Robert Dane. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies operating in the same sector and stage. Key private comparables include Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B).

The peer-median method was applied to the 7 same-sector, same-stage private comparables, yielding a median valuation of $1.0B with a range spanning $300M to $3.0B. This method anchors the primary triangulation given the available data set. No revenue multiples, DCF analysis, or venture capital method inputs were provided, limiting the analysis to relative valuation.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M to $1.1B, with a central estimate of $1.0B. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include the timing and revenue impact of the A$176M Australian Program of Record contract secured in March 2026, which may warrant upward adjustment if viewed as reducing execution risk. The wide peer range ($300M–$3.0B) also indicates material dispersion within the defense tech cohort, suggesting that Ocius's specific differentiation, contract defensibility, and path to scale warrant scrutiny beyond peer medians alone.
